authoritative_surfaces.role: Remote GitHub job graph and exact check-name source of truth.
Accepted architecture rule for the minimum local CI-parity contract.
Maintainer-facing branch-protection guidance that lists the exact required checks.

derivative_surfaces.role: Local mirror of the CI contract and production-group taxonomy.
Workspace task wrapper for the canonical local parity command.
Workflow documentation that routes issue execution and merge automation through local parity plus pager-free polling.
Resolve workflow contract for local CI-equivalent validation before PR creation.
Merge workflow contract for pager-free GitHub polling and merge readiness.
Approved-queue polling contract that continues automatically while CI is pending.
One-shot pager-free GitHub issue/PR/check helper with watch_mode=false.
Regression-lock surface for workflow and documentation contracts.

shadow_policy_findings.reason: Branch-protection setup lags the documented/current seven-check contract and writes temp state outside the repo-owned .tmp boundary.
missing_documented_checks: Internal Production Gate — Docker Parity & Recovery Proofs

required_checks: Internal Production Gate — Docker Parity & Recovery Proofs

aggregate_gate.name: Internal Production Gate — Docker Parity & Recovery Proofs

hang_risks.risk: Default and fresh-checkout local parity subprocesses rely on subprocess.run without a timeout wrapper.
Git ref/worktree helper calls use subprocess.run without a timeout.
The promoted Docker E2E runtime-proof lane uses a direct subprocess.run call with no timeout.
The workflow requires polling until checks reach terminal state, but the caller must add the actual watchdog bound.

bounded_wait_paths.notes: Service reachability loop with a 30-second deadline and 1-second sleep interval.
Subprocess retries are capped by an attempts counter and a configured delay.
